title: DIY Wine Rack Book Ends
summary: What's better than a wine rack?
description: What's better than a wine rack? A wine rack that does more than just hold wine! Create this pair of multifunctional Wine Rack Book Ends in just a few easy steps and for right under $20 in lumber!

steps.title: Check Dimensions & Cut List
steps.body: The plans for this project outline the steps to make one of the two wine holders in the set. To make a set of two, be sure to follow all steps twice. Check all dimensions and the cut list before starting.

steps.title: Create Holes
steps.body: Drill holes into all of the faces of your wine racks using a 3 1/2" hole saw. Use a ruler to find the center of each face.

steps.title: Add Back Panel
steps.body: Add the 10" tall back panel to the wine rack. Be sure that you have cut the top of the panel at a 45 degree angle (with the longest side being the 10" side). Attach the panel using pocket 1 1/4" pocket holes screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Add Bottom Panel
steps.body: Attach the panel using pocket holes, 1 1/4" pocket holes screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Fit Bottom Panel
steps.body: Make sure to dry fit the back panel prior to attaching. One edge should be cut at 45 degrees, making the longest edge 9 1/4" long.

steps.title: Finishing Touches
steps.body: Use wood putty to fill pocket holes. Once dry, sand, and then stain your rack.
